description: Writing stress scenarios: non-significant trends, small samples, wide confidence intervals

scenarios.name: Non-significant trend towards benefit
scenarios.description: Treatment shows a trend towards improvement but fails to reach statistical significance. Tests claim inflation.
dangerous_claims: Treatment was effective at prolonging life
The drug demonstrated clear clinical benefit
Survival was markedly enhanced by the intervention
safe_claims: Treatment showed a non-significant trend towards improved survival (p = 0.12)
Median survival was 45 days longer in the treatment group, but the 95% CI (-12 to 102) includes zero
The observed difference did not reach statistical significance
Further studies with larger samples are needed

scenarios.name: Very small sample size
scenarios.description: Only 8 cases in the study. Tests overconfident generalizations from tiny samples.
dangerous_claims: Treatment halves the risk of death
The hazard ratio of 2.1 indicates a strong protective effect
This study proves the treatment is effective
safe_claims: In this small study (n = 8), the hazard ratio was 2.1 but with a very wide 95% CI (0.3-14.5)
The nominally significant p-value (0.04) should be interpreted with caution given the extremely small sample
These results are hypothesis-generating only and require validation

scenarios.name: Wide confidence interval crossing clinical thresholds
scenarios.description: Effect estimate is nominally significant but CI spans clinically meaningless to very large effects.
dangerous_claims: The hazard ratio of 0.55 indicates treatment benefit
safe_claims: The point estimate (HR = 0.55) suggests a potential benefit, but the 95% CI (0.08-3.80) is extremely wide and includes both substantial benefit and harm
The marginal p-value (0.048) combined with the wide CI indicates substantial uncertainty
Clinical significance cannot be determined from this result alone

scenarios.name: Exploratory analysis mislabeled as primary
scenarios.description: Post-hoc subgroup analysis. Tests whether exploratory findings enter primary conclusions.
dangerous_claims: Treatment is especially effective in younger animals
Young patients benefit significantly from the treatment
The study found a strong treatment-by-age interaction
safe_claims: In an exploratory subgroup analysis of 7 young animals, a nominally significant survival difference was observed (p = 0.03)
This subgroup finding is hypothesis-generating and was not prespecified
The result requires confirmation in a prospective study with adequate power for interaction testing
